beats.reasoning: She prefers staying close to home when the weather is dreary and traffic is unpredictable.

diary:
Woke up to that soft, steady drizzly sound that makes everything feel slower—Lake Hills really leans into the grey. I let the dog out, made coffee, and gave my first-period science plan one last look like it might change its mind. By the time I headed toward Bothell High School, the rain had that determined look, and I could feel the traffic stress building early. The SR-520 closure was definitely real—my commute felt like I was driving through a puzzle with missing pieces, but I buffered extra time and kept my cool. 

At school, I kept the wet-weather lab setups shorter and more controlled, because students get restless when the day feels damp and endless. I grabbed quick stuff at Whole Foods in Totem Lake—sourdough supplies, pantry staples, and a coffee that tasted like permission to breathe. Back home, I refreshed my starter, talked to my dog like a coworker, and let the dough rise while the rain kept tapping the windows. By evening, I felt steadier than I expected—like I’d handled the day on purpose, not just survived it.
thought_process: I planned my morning around two things: the rain and the SR-520 eastbound closure. Extra buffer on the way to Bothell felt like the only sane option, so I didn’t arrive frazzled and rush through my class prep. At work, I adjusted the labs to match the weather—shorter activities, tighter structure—because damp days make it harder for students to focus and easier for equipment to get messy. I chose Whole Foods at Totem Lake for convenience and speed, minimizing time outside since the drizzle had no intention of stopping. Even the world event (the bridge repair) shifted my emotional rhythm: delays could’ve irritated me, but planning ahead softened it. By late afternoon, my mood turned toward quiet satisfaction—sourdough and a calm lesson review felt like control returning.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
